detailed_analysis: Audit of the user-facing CLI/config/rendering/adoption surface plus three test modules. Overall code health is solid: no hardcoded secrets, no SQL/command injection vectors, no mutable default arguments, no bare-except swallowing, no global mutable state, no cyclic imports across the four production modules, and no star imports. cli.py is a thin argparse-on-top-of-merged_config wrapper (254 lines, no God Class). config.py is similarly disciplined and the per-OS branching is correctly localised. rendering.py is pure-helper as advertised. The MAJOR findings concentrate in adoption.py, which is a 648-line module containing a single class (AdoptionEngine) with ~15 methods covering five distinct responsibilities (adopt, sync, conflict resolve, extend, removal). _propagate_removal in particular is ~75 lines with two nested storage-shape branches and broad except-blocks. adoption.py also performs inline 'from agents_sync.agentic_tool_spec import SharedKeyedMapLayout' in four methods to avoid a circular import — this is an architectural smell that the module masks rather than fixes. A real adoption-overwrite hazard exists: in cli.py the import subcommand passes import_collision_strategy straight through with no preflight that the user's local canonical store has been backed up. Exit codes in cli.py are inconsistent. The message argparse emits for legacy installs interpolates the user's home-relative paths into the log. rendering.py:172 — render_to_agentic_tool — at 49 non-blank lines exceeds the 40-line M2 threshold. adoption.py:_project_to_other_tools (76 lines, cyclomatic ~13) and _propagate_removal (74 lines, cyclomatic ~10) both clear the M2 thresholds. Tests: test_cli_export_import.py asserts exit codes, file existence, and zipfile member names — user-visible. test_config_platform_defaults.py asserts the exact string contents of returned paths. test_antigravity_config.py is the weakest: it heavily inspects internal state via syncer.tool_status.snapshot() and caplog log-record contents.

findings.problem: Module exceeds the 500-line threshold (648 lines). AdoptionEngine holds five distinct responsibilities (adopt, sync, conflict-resolve, extend, removal) plus a privacy/reservation guard cluster.
76 non-blank lines, cyclomatic complexity ~13. Mixes three concerns: (a) loading prior text for frontmatter-preservation, (b) privacy filtering, (c) reservation filtering, (d) the render call itself.
74 lines, two nested storage-shape branches, two except Exception blocks that catch every non-BaseException.

findings.variant: Latent cyclic dependency, masked by in-function imports
findings.problem: The same symbol is re-imported inside four separate methods. Either the import is cheap and belongs at module scope, or there is a real cycle that the in-function form hides.
49 non-blank lines, three sequential storage-shape branches that read like an open-coded dispatch table.

PortableArchiveError is a user-input rejection; OSError is a transient failure. Returning the same '1' for both prevents a calling script from distinguishing 'fix the input' from 'retry the operation'.

The import subcommand defaults to import_collision_strategy='mtime_wins' which CAN silently overwrite a user's local canonical. The CLI does not print a 'about to overwrite N pairs' summary, does not ask for confirmation, and does not require a --force flag for the overwrite strategy.

findings.variant: logging.exception in CLI leaks full absolute paths to default handler
findings.problem: logging.exception() emits the full traceback including absolute filesystem paths. If a user pipes stderr to a bug report or a CI log, every traceback frame leaks their home-directory layout.
20 consecutive maybe_set calls. Each new CLI flag requires touching this function AND the parser AND validate_config AND DEFAULTS — four-place change for one concept.
Import-time evaluation of Path.home() and os.environ. Makes import agents_sync.cli non-deterministic across processes.
Mixed sys.platform / os.name branching in the same expression.
Catches every non-BaseException from apply_slot / archive_move. Will swallow programmer errors (TypeError, AttributeError) the same as I/O errors.

On ANY parse failure the function returns False — meaning 'NOT private' — and the renderer proceeds to overwrite the target. If target_io.parse raises because the target file is malformed, the privacy gate fails OPEN.
Tests assert on log-message substrings ('agentic_tool antigravity', '-> available', 'startup -> unavailable'). These are not user-visible contracts — they are internal log format strings.

findings.variant: Too Many Parameters / structural duplication of CLI
findings.problem: The test must keep a hand-written list of 23 argparse keys in sync with cli.py:build_parser.
expand_path is imported inside both _run_export and _run_import despite cli.py already importing from agents_sync.config at module scope.
config: dict is a primitive-obsession marker. A TypedDict or dataclass for the merged config would let mypy catch typos.

verdict_summary: No CRITICAL findings. Structural debt concentrates in adoption.py (648 lines, AdoptionEngine class with five responsibility clusters and two functions >70 lines) and render_to_agentic_tool (49-line storage-shape dispatch). CLI, config, and the per-OS branching are clean; tests pin user-visible behaviour well except for over-pinned log-substring assertions in test_antigravity_config.py.
